title: Major Bug in v1.1.0
description: I've patched a major bug where Pure Comments would fail for most people. Sorry!
excerpt: David made me aware of a fairly major bug where any Pure Comments instance without set, would fail. This is because I hadn’t moved the slug detection function over from the hosted version I’ve been working on...because I'm an idiot that gets easily confused, and I'm not use to managing multiple complicated code bases. Anyway, I've patched the issue and released v1.1.1 of Pure Comments, which fixes the problem and now slugs are inferred from the post URL when is not set, as documented in the READ...

title: How to Update Pure Comments
description: A look at how to update Pure Comments from inside the admin screen.
excerpt: As of v1.1.0 Pure Comments allows you to update direct from the dashboard. To do this, visit and you will be greeted by a page similar to this: Once there you can check if there's an update by hitting the button. Pure Comments will then check the latest release on GitHub to see if you're up to date or not. If you're not, it will tell you: If an update is available, hit the button. Pure Comments will provide a list of what will change during this update for you to review and ensure it won't break...

title: Managing Comments
description: Here's how you actually use Pure Comments once you start receiving comments.
excerpt: So you've managed to setup Pure Comments and you've customised it's theme so it looks beautiful with your blog. Now you need to know how to actually manage comments. If you've configured email notifications with Amazon SES (strongly advised), you will receive a notification when someone leaves a comment on one of your posts that looks like this: The bottom of the notification contains a link to go and moderate the comment, which after logging in will show in the pending comments section: From he...

title: Theming Pure Comments
description: Instructions on how to customise the look of Pure Comments on your site.
excerpt: The Pure Comments package comes with a stylesheet that can be used as a starting point for you to customise its look so it matches the design of your blog. First, copy the example stylesheet either into your existing blog's CSS, or into a separate file that's only loaded on pages where your comments live: From here you can edit to fit the style of your blog's design. If you choose not to update , this is what Pure Comments will look like using the default file: Light mode Dark mode

title: Working with Data Attributes
description: Setting up data attributes for defining slugs and API calls.
excerpt: When embedding Pure Comments, you can keep setup minimal and let infer values automatically, or you can override behaviour with data attributes. Quick Start (No Overrides) If you do this: Then will: infer the post slug from the current page URL infer the API base URL from the script For many sites, that default behaviour is all you need. data post slug (Optional) You can use on the container when you want to explicitly control the post/thread identifier. When to use it Your article URL might cha...

title: Getting Started
description: Instructions on how to get started with Pure Comments.
excerpt: Pure Comments has been designed from the ground up to be simple to self host and embed on your personal blog. Installation and setup is just a few steps: 1. Download the latest souce code. 2. Upload it to your server. 3. Add the Pure Comments embed code to your blog. 4. Enjoy!
